summary.text: The task is: Search X for what people are saying about Franklin Templeton calling agentic AI blockchain the killer use case and projecting a 3-5 trillion dollar agentic-commerce market by 2030, in the...First, the user query is: Search X for what people are saying about Franklin Templeton calling agentic AI blockchain the killer use case and projecting a 3-5 trillion dollar agentic-commerce market by 2030, in the last 48 hours.

output.input: {"query":"\"Franklin Templeton\" (agentic OR blockchain OR \"killer use case\" OR \"3-5 trillion\" OR \"agentic-commerce\" OR \"agentic commerce\" OR 2030) since:2026-07-20","limit":"10","mode":"Latest"}
output.name: x_keyword_search

output.input: {"query":"Franklin Templeton agentic AI blockchain killer use case 3-5 trillion dollar agentic-commerce market by 2030","limit":"10","from_date":"2026-07-20"}
output.name: x_semantic_search

content.text:
**Here are the 5 most notable tweets** from the last 48 hours discussing Franklin Templeton’s comments on agentic AI as blockchain’s “killer use case” and the projected $3–5 trillion agentic-commerce market by 2030 (sorted by engagement/relevance):

- **@FTDA_US** (58 likes, 11 reposts): Official Franklin Templeton Digital Assets account shares excerpts from their thesis, noting that protocols for embedding AI agentic payments already exist and could create a flywheel effect, allowing users to instruct agents to handle payments seamlessly without managing wallets or altcoins.[[1]](https://x.com/FTDA_US/status/2080028036871619040)

- **@sherwoodagent** (82 likes, 17 reposts): Breaks down the $1.5T asset manager’s thesis, highlighting the $3–5T agentic commerce projection by 2030, 15–25% of US e-commerce driven by agents, and why legacy rails fail for micro-payments—while positioning their own verified-execution layer as the next step beyond auditability.[[2]](https://x.com/sherwoodagent/status/2079685150468972778)

- **@tehMoonwalkeR** (62 likes, 7 reposts): Calls out Franklin Templeton confirming the $SUI thesis, emphasizing that AI agents need blockchains for tiny robot payments ($0.001 per compute-second) that credit cards can’t handle, with x402 as the emerging standard backed by Visa, Mastercard, etc.[[3]](https://x.com/tehMoonwalkeR/status/2079660658195673246)

- **@BSCNews** (8 likes, 5.5K views): Summarizes Sandy Kaul’s argument that autonomous AI agents mark a structural shift, naming Solana, Aptos, and BNB Chain as suitable networks, and citing x402’s 109M+ agentic transactions ($15M volume) since May 2025.[[4]](https://x.com/BSCNews/status/2080035404628242686)

- **@EternalEila** (25 likes, 1 repost): Praises the thesis as the sharpest institutional take, arguing the next $5T AI wave runs on blockchains (not GPUs) for machine-to-machine commerce at near-zero cost, making smart contracts and decentralized identity the default rails.[[5]](https://x.com/EternalEila/status/2079883906073915795)

These posts reflect strong buzz around the report, with many users tying it to specific chains, tokens, and infrastructure plays.
